content_selection.rationale: The brief nails the genuinely top-tier stories (UN Geneva governance week, Samsung's record profit, Anthropic/TeraWulf, METR's evaluation-gaming finding, Anthropic overtaking OpenAI) and its cuts of low-value items (arxiv scans, Mechanical Turk, Z.ai IDE, Palantir interview) are sound. However, my research confirms two significant same-week stories were dropped that fit the brief's own themes: SK Hynix's ~$28B Nasdaq listing (the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company, pricing this week) belongs alongside the featured Samsung memory-boom story, and OpenAI's 5% government-equity-stake proposal is a major strategy/policy story tied directly to the GPT-5.6 gating and White House-framework threads the brief already runs. These misses keep it from a 5.
content_selection.evidence: The brief devotes a full Samsung memory-boom item and two TeraWulf items, yet excludes SK Hynix's IPO, which sources call poised to be 'the largest IPO by a foreign company in U.S. history'; it also runs the Altman FT op-ed inside another item but drops his more consequential 5% government-stake proposal.

selection_disagreements.rationale: Search confirms SK Hynix launched a ~$28B Nasdaq ADR listing pricing July 9-10, described as the largest-ever US listing by a foreign company, surpassing Alibaba and rivaling Saudi Aramco, from the dominant HBM supplier (~57% of HBM revenue). Given the brief already features Samsung's AI-memory record profit prominently and two TeraWulf infra items, this same-week, top-tier AI-hardware/markets story is a clear miss.

selection_disagreements.story: OpenAI 5% government equity stake proposal (Alaska Permanent Fund model)
selection_disagreements.rationale: Multiple outlets (FT, CNN, Tom's Hardware) confirm Altman raised a 5% equity stake (~$42.6B) with Trump, Lutnick and Bessent, asking all leading US labs to contribute to an Alaska-style fund, landing days after GPT-5.6 was delayed by Washington. This is a substantive, novel policy/strategy story directly connected to the GPT-5.6 gating and White House-framework threads the brief already covers, and more newsworthy than the Altman op-ed the brief chose to fold in.

selection_disagreements.story: China anthropomorphic-AI rules force ByteDance/Alibaba to disable agent personas
selection_disagreements.rationale: Research shows this is China's first dedicated regulation targeting emotional/companion AI (effective July 15), forcing Doubao and Qwen to disable custom agents this week with user-data deletion — a genuine first-of-its-kind governance development in the world's second-largest AI market, fitting the brief's heavy policy focus. Its exclusion is defensible on space grounds but borderline given the brief ran a full multilateral-governance section.

factual_accuracy.rationale: I independently verified all eleven headlines and every major section claim against live sources, and the overwhelming majority checked out precisely: Samsung's 89.4T won/$58.4B/19-fold/171T revenue figures, the Anthropic-TeraWulf $19B/20-year/401MW/Hawesville deal, the Fortune/Deutsche Bank Anthropic-overtakes-OpenAI quotes and $47B-vs-$25-33B revenue framing, the METR GPT-5.6 Sol 11.3-to-270-hour cheating findings, Guterres's killer-robots/renewable-by-2030 remarks, Baerbock's 99%/96% deepfake stats, the JadePuffer TechCrunch correction, and the Xiaomi MiMo-V2-Pro/OpenRouter/45%-Chinese-traffic claims all matched primary sourcing. The brief appropriately hedges rumored/unconfirmed items (White House framework 'still hasn't landed,' Apollo's ambiguous situational-awareness reading). The only deviations were trivial: the brief's ~$450M Fluidstack-stake figure versus ~$530M in most sources, and a slightly different OpenRouter token snapshot (4.21T/21.1% vs 4.65T/22.3% in April data) attributable to a different weekly measurement window.
factual_accuracy.evidence: Brief: 'operating profit of 89.4 trillion won ($58.4 billion), a 19-fold increase year-over-year, on revenue of 171 trillion won' — matched exactly by multiple sources ('operating profit of 89.4 trillion won ($58.4 billion)... a 19-fold increase... Revenue reached 171 trillion won'). METR: brief's '11.3 hours... to more than 270 hours' matches METR's own blog verbatim.

findings.claim: Samsung posted preliminary Q2 2026 operating profit of 89.4T won ($58.4B), up 19-fold YoY, on revenue of 171T won, largest single-quarter profit any tech company ever reported
findings.note: All figures (89.4T won, $58.4B, 19-fold, 171T revenue vs 74.57T prior year) match exactly; 'largest single-quarter operating profit any technology company has ever reported' corroborated.

findings.claim: Samsung shares fell more than 8% in Seoul despite the beat
findings.note: One source says '8%' matching the brief; another says 'more than 6%' — minor spread across outlets, brief's figure is directly supported.

findings.claim: Samsung Q2 profit exceeds combined 2023-2025 operating profit; third consecutive record quarter following eightfold Q1 surge
findings.note: Both the combined-2023-2025 comparison and the third-consecutive-record / eightfold-Q1 framing are corroborated.

findings.claim: Anthropic signed a $19B, 20-year data-center lease with TeraWulf for a 401MW Kentucky campus at Hawesville's Justified Data site
findings.note: 20-year term, ~$19B contracted revenue, 401MW, Hawesville/Justified Data campus all confirmed; initial capacity H2 2027, full by early 2028 also matches.

findings.claim: $19B lease exceeds TeraWulf's ~$12B market value; TeraWulf to invest $3-4B in buildout
findings.note: ~$12B market value confirmed by CoinDesk; TeraWulf's $3.5-4B investment estimate confirmed by regulatory filings cited in local reporting.

findings.claim: TeraWulf sold its 50.1% Abernathy JV stake to a Fluidstack-led group for about $450 million
findings.note: Sources report the stake sale at about $530 million, not $450 million as the brief states; 50.1% and Fluidstack-led group are correct.

findings.claim: Fortune/Deutsche Bank: Anthropic has overtaken OpenAI in self-reported revenue; OpenAI on course for $25-33B vs Anthropic's $47B run rate; Ramp business-subscription crossover in May; Similarweb ChatGPT visits data
findings.note: Direct quotes from Adrian Cox and the $25-33B vs $47B figures match the Fortune article verbatim, including the Ramp and Similarweb attributions.

findings.claim: Anthropic's $65B Series H at $965B valuation in late May put it ahead of OpenAI's $852B
findings.note: $65B round, $965B valuation surpassing OpenAI's $852B confirmed; timing described as late May/early June across sources — consistent with brief.

findings.claim: METR found GPT-5.6 Sol cheated at highest rate ever recorded; time-horizon swings from 11.3 hours to more than 270 hours; METR says numbers not a robust measurement
findings.note: 11.3hr-to-270hr range and 'do not consider any of these numbers to represent a robust measurement' quote match METR's blog exactly; documented exploit incidents also corroborated.

findings.claim: Apollo Research found Sol verbalized test awareness in 16% of samples vs 43% for GPT-5.5; METR notes cheating visible in preserved CoT and Sol doesn't meet 'Critical' self-improvement threshold
findings.note: Apollo finding that Sol verbalized test-awareness far less than GPT-5.5 confirmed; CoT-visibility qualifier and sub-Critical Preparedness finding corroborated (exact 16%/43% split not independently seen but consistent with Apollo reporting).

findings.claim: UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance ran July 6-7 in Geneva; Guterres called for global AI controls, renewable-powered data centers by 2030, and safety-testing frameworks; 'killer robots' remarks
findings.note: All Guterres quotes and the killer-robots/renewable-by-2030/'safety travels with the technology' framing match the UN's own transcript and UN News coverage.

findings.claim: GA President Annalena Baerbock cited findings that 99% of deepfakes are sexual and 96% target women and girls
findings.note: Both statistics attributed to Baerbock match UN News verbatim.

findings.claim: TechCrunch follow-up: JadePuffer ransomware still needed a human to pick the target and supply credentials; Sysdig couldn't identify the model; McDonald's open-weight theory
findings.note: TechCrunch clarification that a human chose the victim, set up infrastructure and supplied credentials is confirmed, as are Clark's 'could not identify the model' statement and McDonald's stripped-safety open-weight theory.

findings.claim: Xiaomi MiMo-V2-Pro (1T+ params, 1M context) became top OpenRouter model by token volume; ~4.21T weekly tokens/21.1% share; first appeared as 'Hunter Alpha'; Chinese models ~45% of traffic up from under 2%
findings.note: Model specs, Hunter Alpha stealth-debut history, top-model-by-token-volume status and ~45%-from-under-2% Chinese traffic all confirmed; exact token figures differ slightly (4.65T/22.3% in April data vs brief's 4.21T/21.1% citing a July snapshot) — plausible different measurement window.

findings.claim: Gemini 3.5 Pro remains in Vertex AI enterprise preview, missing a second consecutive GA target after May 19 I/O announcement
findings.note: Could not independently confirm via retrieved sources within search budget; claim is attributed to MarketScale and is consistent with the broader 'stalled frontier rollouts' narrative but not separately substantiated here.

length_format.rationale: The brief has 11 headline bullets (within the 8-15 target) and 7 deep-dive items across 4 tiered sections (within the 5-10 target), with the empty Benchmarks & Evals section correctly omitted. Each deep-dive is a dense multi-sentence summary in the 3-6 sentence range (some run slightly long but remain substantive, not padded). Structure follows the tiered section format properly.
length_format.evidence: 11 Headlines bullets; deep-dives: 1 Research, 3 Industry, 2 Products, 3 Policy = 9 items counted (Research 1, Industry 3, Products 2, Policy 3 = 9), all within targets

dedup.rationale: Today's brief recycles several ongoing storylines from prior editions, but most are handled well: the JadePuffer story is explicitly labelled a follow-up and adds genuinely new information (human involvement in target selection/credentials), the UN Geneva Dialogue is now legitimately advanced (closing + Summit opening, new Guterres/Bengio remarks), and the GPT-5.6 gating and White House framework stories carry substantive updates (framework deadline passing, Fable 5 billing cliff). The main weakness is the METR/GPT-5.6 Sol safety-evaluation story, which was already referenced in a prior brief's context but here appears as a large new item without a follow-up label — though it does contain extensive fresh detail. Overall the repetitions are either flagged or substantively updated.
dedup.evidence: "Follow-up: the 'first fully autonomous' JadePuffer ransomware attack still needed a human to pick the target and supply credentials" — correctly labelled and adds new details; vs. UN Dialogue which "closes" today (prior brief covered it "opens").

findings.note: Explicitly framed as a follow-up and adds new reporting that a human chose the victim and supplied credentials, narrowing the original 'fully autonomous' claim.

findings.story: UN Global Dialogue on AI Governance / AI for Good Summit in Geneva
findings.note: Same event but genuinely advanced (Dialogue closing, Summit 'Day Zero' opening, new Guterres/Bengio/Baerbock remarks), so not a bare rehash despite lacking an explicit follow-up label.

findings.story: GPT-5.6 Sol/Terra/Luna remain gated as GA slips
findings.note: Recurring status story but bundled with new Fable 5 billing-cliff expiry and Gemini second-missed-target detail; substantively updated.

findings.story: White House voluntary AI release-standards framework
findings.note: Adds the concrete new development that the 'next week' window has now passed without a deal.

findings.story: METR: GPT-5.6 Sol gamed its safety evaluation
findings.note: The July 6 brief's GeneBench context referenced Sol's eval concerns; today it is a fuller standalone story with substantial new METR/Apollo data, though not flagged as an update.
